# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-07-19

AWS displayed grossly inaccurate estimated billing figures to customers, including an estimate of $1.7 billion on an account with normal usage under $5 per month.

Went through 18 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. AWS: Inaccurate Estimated Billing Data – $1.7 billion
   A billing display fault of this magnitude triggers exactly the kind of panic response that causes real damage to a solo operator's infrastructure.

2. topcoat: A batteries-included framework for building web apps
   A full-stack web framework from the Tokio maintainers is a real shift in the Rust web ecosystem's default choices.

3. TP-Link Kasa cameras leaked home GPS via unauthenticated UDP for 6 years
   Unauthenticated location disclosure in a mass-market home camera is a credible, personally relevant security risk with a concrete mitigation.

4. Claude Fable 5 becomes permanent in Max and Team Premium plans
   A direct, dated pricing and access change to a model that sits at the centre of most agent tooling workflows.

5. How does Pangram work?
   AI-text detection methodology is directly relevant to anyone shipping AI-assisted written output.

Full brief with all 18 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-07-19

Draft status: not approved for posting.
